feature: Dogfood: Seed bop's own backlog as bop cards
workflow_rationale: Single-service task with no complex dependencies. Just creating cards and writing specs - no multi-phase coordination needed.

phases.name: Create and populate cards
phases.description: Create 7 cards across 4 teams with real specifications

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "watchpaths-dispatcher") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# WatchPaths Dispatcher Extension

## Overview
Extend the dispatcher's WatchPaths mechanism to monitor all team-*/pending directories, not just a single pending/ directory. This is a follow-on to spec 010.

## Requirements
- Watch all team-arch/pending, team-cli/pending, team-quality/pending, team-platform/pending, team-intelligence/pending directories
- Trigger dispatcher when any .bop directory appears in any team's pending/ folder
- Maintain single-threaded dispatcher guarantee (no concurrent dispatches per team)

## Scope
- IN: Extend WatchPaths event source in dispatcher.rs
- IN: Update launchd plist to watch multiple directories
- OUT: Does NOT change card movement logic or state machine

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dispatcher fires when card dropped in any team-*/pending/ directory
- [ ] `cargo test` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] No regression in existing pending/ directory monitoring
subtasks.notes: Created watchpaths-dispatcher card in .cards/team-arch/pending/ with complete spec.md describing the WatchPaths extension requirements. Card includes proper meta.json with heart glyph (🂱) for team-arch, Info.plist, links.md, and prompt.md template. Verified with `cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending` showing the card is properly indexed.

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "jj-gitignore-audit") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# JJ Gitignore Audit

## Overview
Audit the repository for large files that cause `jj snapshot` to fail or hang, and add appropriate patterns to .gitignore to prevent them from being tracked.

## Requirements
- Identify all files >10MB that are tracked or could be tracked
- Find common patterns for build artifacts, logs, temp files
- Add patterns to .gitignore that prevent jj from tracking these
- Verify `jj st` completes in <2 seconds after changes

## Scope
- IN: Audit target/, logs/, .workspaces/, QuickLook/ directories
- IN: Update .gitignore with size-based and pattern-based exclusions
- IN: Test that jj snapshot works without hanging
- OUT: Does NOT migrate existing jj history or rewrite commits

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .gitignore includes patterns for all large file categories
- [ ] `jj st` completes in <2 seconds
- [ ] No large files (>10MB) appear in `jj st` output
- [ ] Build artifacts are properly ignored
subtasks.notes: Created jj-gitignore-audit card in .cards/team-arch/pending/ with complete spec.md describing the jj .gitignore audit requirements. Card includes proper meta.json with heart glyph (🂲) for team-arch, Info.plist, links.md, and prompt.md template. Spec covers auditing large files (>1MB) that break jj snapshot, adding patterns to .gitignore, and verifying clean jj status. Verified with `cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json` showing the card is properly indexed with id "jj-gitignore-audit".

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "bop-serve") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# Bop Serve HTTP Endpoint

## Overview
Add `bop serve` command that runs an HTTP server accepting POST requests with spec content to create cards. This enables the ai-coding-job.yml GitHub Actions pattern without requiring self-hosted runners.

## Requirements
- HTTP endpoint at POST /cards accepting JSON: {"id": "card-id", "template": "implement", "spec": "markdown content", "team": "cli"}
- Create card in pending/ directory with provided spec
- Return 201 with card path on success, 400/409 on validation errors
- Support authentication via bearer token from environment variable

## Scope
- IN: New `bop serve` subcommand in main.rs
- IN: HTTP server using warp or axum
- IN: Card creation via existing create_card() function
- OUT: Does NOT include dispatcher integration (cards sit in pending/)
- OUT: Does NOT include GitHub webhook handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bop serve --port 8080` starts HTTP server
- [ ] POST /cards creates valid card in pending/
- [ ] Invalid requests return appropriate error codes
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with new integration tests
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
subtasks.notes: Created bop-serve card in .cards/pending/🂢-bop-serve.bop/ using `bop new implement bop-serve --team team-cli`. Updated spec.md with comprehensive requirements for HTTP endpoint that accepts spec body via POST to create cards, enabling ai-coding-job.yml pattern without self-hosted runners. Card includes: JSON request format, validation/error handling, basic auth token support, configurable host:port, graceful shutdown, and comprehensive acceptance criteria. Verified card is indexed correctly with id "bop-serve" using verification command.

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "bop-doctor-checks") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# Bop Doctor Enhanced Checks

## Overview
Expand `bop doctor` command to check for required tools (nu, jj), adapter availability, and launchd plist status. Provide actionable diagnostics.

## Requirements
- Check `nu --version` exists and is >= 0.111.0
- Check `jj --version` exists and is functional
- Verify all adapters/*.nu files exist and are executable
- Check launchd plist status: loaded, enabled, running
- Report missing dependencies with installation instructions

## Scope
- IN: Extend doctor.rs with version checks
- IN: Add adapter availability scan (check adapters/*.nu paths)
- IN: Add launchd plist status check via `launchctl list`
- OUT: Does NOT auto-fix issues (just reports them)
- OUT: Does NOT install missing dependencies

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bop doctor` reports nu version status
- [ ] `bop doctor` reports jj version status
- [ ] `bop doctor` lists available adapters
- [ ] `bop doctor` shows launchd plist status
- [ ] `cargo test` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
subtasks.notes: Created bop-doctor-checks card with spec for expanding bop doctor to check nu version, jj version, adapter availability, and launchd plist status. Card uses 🂡 (ace of spades) glyph for team-cli. Verification passed: bop list shows the card correctly.

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "e2e-factory-test") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# E2E Factory Test

## Overview
Create end-to-end integration test verifying that `bop factory install` + dropping a card triggers dispatcher and moves card to done/ without manual daemon management.

## Requirements
- Test script that runs: factory install → create test card → verify dispatcher fires → check card in done/
- Clean setup/teardown (unload plist before, restore after)
- Use mock adapter for predictable behavior
- Test should complete in <30 seconds

## Scope
- IN: New integration test in tests/ or scripts/
- IN: Verify launchd WatchPaths triggers dispatcher
- IN: Verify card state transitions (pending → running → done)
- OUT: Does NOT test real adapters (use mock only)
- OUT: Does NOT test merge-gate or PR creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test creates card, factory triggers dispatcher automatically
- [ ] Card moves from pending/ to running/ to done/
- [ ] Test cleans up launchd plist after run
- [ ] Test passes on clean macOS system
- [ ] Test script is documented and runnable via `make`
subtasks.notes: Created e2e-factory-test card in .cards/pending/🃁-e2e-factory-test.bop/ with comprehensive spec.md describing the end-to-end factory test requirements. Card includes proper meta.json with diamond glyph (🃁) for team-quality, Info.plist, links.md, and prompt.md template. Spec covers testing the complete workflow: bop factory install → drop card → dispatcher fires → card moves to done/ without manual intervention. Verified with verification command showing card is properly indexed with id "e2e-factory-test". Card committed to repository with proper git commit message.

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "adapter-stress-test") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# Adapter Stress Test

## Overview
Run 10 cards concurrently through the ollama adapter to verify no race conditions, proper PID tracking, and correct orphan reaper behavior under load.

## Requirements
- Spawn 10 cards simultaneously using dispatcher with --max-workers 10
- All cards use ollama-local adapter
- Verify no PID collisions or lost processes
- Confirm orphan reaper handles all child processes correctly
- Check for file descriptor leaks or resource exhaustion

## Scope
- IN: Stress test script using dispatcher with high concurrency
- IN: Verify reaper.rs correctly tracks all spawned PIDs
- IN: Check no cards stuck in running/ after test
- OUT: Does NOT test non-ollama adapters
- OUT: Does NOT test network failure scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 cards complete successfully (in done/ or failed/)
- [ ] No cards remain in running/ after dispatcher exits
- [ ] No orphaned ollama processes remain (`ps aux | grep ollama`)
- [ ] Test completes in <5 minutes
- [ ] Test logs show clean PID lifecycle for all cards
subtasks.notes: Created adapter-stress-test card in .cards/pending/🃂-adapter-stress-test.bop/ with comprehensive spec.md describing requirements for stress testing the ollama adapter with 10 concurrent cards. Card includes proper meta.json with diamond glyph (🃂) for team-quality, Info.plist, links.md, and prompt.md template. Spec covers concurrent dispatch with --max-workers 10, PID tracking verification, orphan reaper behavior, and resource leak checks. Verified with verification command showing card is properly indexed with id "adapter-stress-test". Card committed to repository with proper git commit message.

verification.command: cargo run --bin bop -- list --state pending --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id == "model-routing") | .id'

subtasks.spec_content:
# Model Routing by Cost

## Overview
Route cards to cheap models (claude-haiku) vs capable models (claude-sonnet/opus) based on a `cost` field in meta.json. This optimizes spend for simple vs complex tasks.

## Requirements
- Add optional `cost` field to meta.json: "cheap" | "capable" | "premium"
- Default to "capable" if not specified
- Map cost to model selection: cheap=haiku, capable=sonnet, premium=opus
- Dispatcher selects model based on cost field before invoking adapter

## Scope
- IN: Add cost field to Meta struct in bop-core/src/meta.rs
- IN: Add model routing logic in dispatcher.rs
- IN: Update templates to include cost field
- OUT: Does NOT change existing provider_chain or stage_models logic
- OUT: Does NOT add dynamic cost estimation (manual field only)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] meta.json supports "cost" field
- [ ] Cards with cost="cheap" use claude-haiku
- [ ] Cards with cost="capable" use claude-sonnet (default)
- [ ] Cards with cost="premium" use claude-opus
- [ ] `cargo test` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
subtasks.notes: Created model-routing card in .cards/team-intelligence/pending/🃓-model-routing.bop/ with comprehensive spec.md describing requirements for routing cards to different model tiers based on cost field in meta.json. Card includes proper meta.json with club glyph (🃓) for team-intelligence, Info.plist, links.md, and prompt.md template. Spec covers adding optional cost field (cheap/capable/premium), model selection logic in dispatcher, and template updates. Verified with verification command showing card is properly indexed with id "model-routing".

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk - only creating card files, not modifying code. Command-based verification is sufficient.
